results.editorial:
Selangor King George V Silver Jubilee Fund (SKGV) is one of Malaysia's oldest charitable care homes, established in 1937 in Pudu, Kuala Lumpur, to shelter destitute Chinese women who had migrated from China in the 1930s and found themselves sleeping on five-foot-ways. More than 85 years on, it remains rooted in the same mission: providing a home for elderly women aged 65 and above who are homeless, have no surviving dependents, and are broadly capable of self-care. The facility, registered with social welfare authorities under W/PJB WT 001/2017, sits at 3052 Jalan Jubilee, Pudu, and is run with a lean staff supplemented heavily by public donations and volunteer groups.

SKGV accommodates around 60 residents according to Google listing data. Care sits at the independent-to-low-dependency end of the spectrum — residents can largely look after themselves, with the home providing meals, shelter, and a community. Documented activities include vegetable gardening, daily Qigong exercise sessions, and regular visits from volunteer groups who conduct free health screenings and basic medical check-ups. Operational funding is donation-driven, covering groceries, medical aids, and consumables. The facility received a visit from the Prime Minister in February 2022, indicating some degree of public recognition.

Families looking at SKGV need to understand this is fundamentally a charitable refuge for women without family support — it is unlikely to accept residents who have family networks or who require nursing-level medical care, catheter management, dementia supervision, or specialist intervention. No private or semi-private pricing structure has been published. The contact person on record is Ms Fannie Poon (010-280 3380; office: 03-9223 4891). Ask directly about admission criteria, what medical care is available on-site versus referred out, and whether places are available given the modest published capacity.

facts.religion: No stated affiliation; historically Chinese community
facts.red_flags: Donation-funded operations; care level limited to semi-independent residents; female-only admission; no nursing care confirmed

results.skip_reason: Minimal verifiable information found. Google Maps places this in Seksyen 7, Shah Alam (phone 03-7958 5055) but no website, Facebook page, or directory listing with confirmed details was located. One directory cross-references a 'Suria Care Centre' in Titiwangsa KL — likely a different facility sharing a similar name. With a 3.5-star average from only 6 reviews and no public content to draw from, there is insufficient verified material for a responsible editorial.

facts.red_flags: Very low review rating (3.5/5); no public web presence found; possible name confusion with Titiwangsa facility

results.skip_reason: No website, Facebook page, or substantive directory listing could be located for City Home Care Centre in Taman Chi Liung, Klang. The facility does not appear in any nursing home roundup for Klang and has only 6 Google reviews averaging 1.7 stars — the lowest in this batch. Without any independently verifiable information about ownership, services, staffing, or care level, and given the very poor rating, there is insufficient material for an accurate editorial.

facts.red_flags: Critically low Google rating of 1.7/5 from 6 reviews — strongly recommend verifying current operational status before considering; no public web presence found

results.editorial:
Woodrose Senior Residences is a growing Shah Alam-based operator that emerged from the merger of Zara Aisyah Care Centre (ZACC) and MLL Sdn Bhd, positioning itself as Selangor's leading Syariah-compliant elderly care brand. The Seksyen 13 branch — listed as Woodrose Senior Residences 3, reachable at +60 12-709 8102 — is specifically designated as a female-only, Syariah-compliant residence, making it one of the few nursing facilities in the Klang Valley explicitly designed for Muslim women who require religious and gender-sensitive care. The head office sits in Seksyen 9, Shah Alam, with other branches in Seksyen 11.

Woodrose's Syariah compliance framework is documented on their website and covers four pillars: same-gender medical and caregiving teams assigned to female residents; halal-certified food, beverages, and pharmaceuticals; prayer facilities with ablution assistance and tayammum provision for bedridden residents; and management operating on Islamic ethical principles. Across their network, the operator offers residential nursing care, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, physiotherapy, respite care, and senior day care. These services are understood to apply across branches, though the specific clinical capacity of the Seksyen 13 location should be confirmed directly.

For families seeking a female Muslim environment, this is likely the most structured option in Shah Alam. The facility does not publish its bed count or pricing online, so call ahead to confirm availability, room configurations, and what level of nursing dependency they can accommodate at the Seksyen 13 site specifically. Ask whether a resident can receive opposite-gender medical attention in emergencies (their stated dharurah policy allows this with a mahram present), and clarify whether the branch handles bedridden or dementia cases or refers high-dependency residents to their other locations.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite, day care
facts.red_flags: Female-only admission; Syariah compliance specifics per branch require direct confirmation; bed count and pricing not published

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua SRA was founded on 1 January 2020 by Sharil Hussein, a former Ministry of Health civil servant, as a protective shelter for elderly people discharged or abandoned from hospitals whose families cannot provide care. The home is located in Taman Malawati, Kuala Selangor — a suburban setting roughly an hour from central Kuala Lumpur. Residents are drawn predominantly from referrals through the hospital system rather than private placements, reflecting the founder's healthcare background and the NGO's welfare-first mandate. The facility is operated under the Selangor Relief Agency (SRA) umbrella.

As of early 2025, the home was caring for 34 elderly and frail residents. Funding had historically relied on Selangor Zakat Board support of approximately RM1,850 per resident per month, which was subsequently cut to RM800 — precipitating a financial crisis that led to press coverage about potential eviction from their rented bungalow by May 2025. A local welfare NGO (Mayda) subsequently stepped in to assist in identifying alternative premises. The resident profile appears to include bedridden and uzur (severely debilitated) individuals, suggesting the home handles higher-dependency cases beyond basic assisted living.

The financial turbulence reported in 2025 is a serious consideration for families. Before placing a resident here, it is essential to verify the current premises situation and operational continuity, confirm whether Zakat funding has been restored or replaced, and understand the fee structure for non-asnaf residents. The phone number on record is +60 19-879 1926. Ask specifically about the care team composition, whether nursing staff are available overnight, and what happens to residents if the home faces another funding shortfall.

facts.care_types: Nursing home / welfare shelter (frail and bedridden elderly)
facts.red_flags: Severe financial crisis reported January 2025 — risk of eviction; Zakat funding cut; operational continuity must be verified before placement; rented premises

facts.red_flags: No public web presence; care-level and staffing unknown; small review count limits reliability of rating

results.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Damai — known in Chinese as 乐善社老人残障中心 (Lok Sin Society Elderly and Disabled Centre) — is a registered NGO care home located at No. 30, Jalan Damai Perdana 2/6B, Bandar Damai Perdana, Cheras, on the Selangor–Kuala Lumpur fringe. The Chinese name signals a community-welfare orientation rooted in traditional voluntary sector charity rather than private commercial operation. The home serves a mixed elderly and physically disabled population, with up to approximately 100 residents documented across available sources. Operating hours are listed as daily 10:30am–5:00pm for visits, though residential care is around the clock.

The home received support from the Sin Chew Daily Foundation's 300万送暖 (RM3 million warming) programme, indicating recognition from within the Chinese-medium philanthropic community. The facility appears to accommodate residents at varying dependency levels — both ambulatory elderly and more dependent individuals with physical disabilities — suggesting a broader intake than most nursing homes. No clinical specialisations such as dementia units or formal rehabilitation programmes have been confirmed from available public sources.

As an NGO home, pricing is likely means-tested or donation-supplemented rather than fixed at commercial rates, but this should be confirmed directly (contact: +60 17-370 0403; Facebook: wargatuadamai). The Chinese-language community orientation means Mandarin and likely Cantonese are working languages, though the name Damai and the Cheras location suggest some Malay-speaking capacity too. Families should ask about the ratio of nursing-trained staff to caregivers, whether residents with dementia or complex medical needs are accepted, and how fee structures work for residents without family financial support.

facts.care_types: Nursing home / NGO welfare home (elderly and disabled)
facts.religion: No stated affiliation; Chinese community NGO
facts.red_flags: NGO-run with donation-dependent funding; clinical staffing level unconfirmed; visiting hours limited though residential care is 24/7

results.skip_reason: Despite an active Google presence and a distinguishing Chinese name (梳邦再也疗养院), no website, Facebook page, or directory listing could be positively matched to this specific facility at the Putra Heights location. Search results for 'Emerald Nursing Care Home' in Subang Jaya surface contact details that cannot be independently verified as belonging to this Google Maps listing. The phone number (+60 18-987 3862) is a mobile with no associated online profile. With only 4 Google reviews at 5.0 stars and no confirmable public content, there is insufficient material for a responsible editorial.

facts.red_flags: No verifiable web presence; phone is mobile-only; unable to confirm identity of facility vs other 'Emerald' entities found online

results.editorial:
Noble Care Retirement Home in Kampung Sungai Sekamat, Kajang sits at Lot 4288, Jalan Cheras BT 12½, roughly 20 minutes by road from Serdang Hospital, KPJ Kajang Specialist Hospital, and Kajang Hospital — a useful proximity for residents with ongoing medical appointments. Noble Care is a multi-branch operator with facilities across the Klang Valley and beyond (Klang, Cheras, Ampang, Sungai Petani), positioning itself at the premium end of the Kajang nursing home market. The Kajang branch is described as offering a 'serene sanctuary' with an emphasis on personalised care alongside lifestyle amenities.

Nursing capabilities at Noble Care Kajang cover a broad clinical range: palliative care, respite care, dementia care, stroke and cancer care, post-operative care, catheter and colostomy management, nasogastric tube feeding, tracheostomy care, bedsore dressing, and physiotherapy. The facility claims 24/7 nursing supervision by qualified nurses and experienced caregivers. Lifestyle amenities listed include a treehouse, gym, zen garden, rooftop terrace, spa, sauna, massage services, and yoga and Tai Chi classes — signalling a resort-style positioning that goes well beyond typical nursing home offerings.

Pricing and bed count are not published on their website; contact them at +60 16-278 6993 or +60 16-778 6795 to request a consultation. The premium positioning implies rates at the higher end of the Kajang market, so budget-conscious families should clarify costs upfront. Ask specifically about the staffing ratio on night shifts, whether the dementia unit is a dedicated locked ward or integrated with general residents, and what the discharge or escalation pathway is if a resident's medical needs exceed what the facility can provide.

facts.care_types: Nursing home, dementia care, palliative care, rehabilitation, respite, stroke care, post-op care
facts.red_flags: Pricing not published; premium positioning may mean higher costs; verify nursing ratios and dementia unit specifics

results.skip_reason: Canaan Home (迦南美地) in Kampung Indah Permai, Ampang has a Facebook page (Canaan118) confirming its existence as an elderly retirement home with a Chinese-language name suggesting a Chinese community or Christian orientation. However, the Facebook content was inaccessible for scraping and no website, directory listing with care details, staffing information, or pricing could be retrieved. With only 4 Google reviews at 4.8 stars and insufficient public content, there is not enough verified information to write a responsible editorial without fabrication.

facts.religion: Likely Christian (name 'Canaan' / 迦南美地 is biblical); unconfirmed
facts.red_flags: No accessible public information on care services, staffing, or pricing; small review base
